mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing output, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of INDPRO growth with 20-day returns falls below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window, or the signal produces negative mean returns in periods of positive INDPRO growth.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals economic contraction, reducing consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points in 3 months
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE has risen by more than 0.3pp in 3 months, the 20-day forward return mean is not negative or the proportion of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) reflect strong consumer demand and labor market tightening, boosting corporate revenues and S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and PAYEMS are increasing month-over-month, the mean 20-day forward return is not significantly greater than zero (one-sample t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In the late-cycle expansion phase when unemployment is already low, further strong payroll gains exacerbate wage inflation and force the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods classified as late-cycle (UNRATE < 5% and PAYEMS rising), the 60-day forward return median is not negative (sign test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly housing starts figure below 1,000,000 units signals a downturn in residential investment, historically preceding economic weakness and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST < 1000, the 60-day forward return median is greater than or equal to zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP (GDPC1) indicates broad economic expansion, increasing corporate earnings and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of GDPC1 growth with 20-day returns is below 0.05 over any 5-year window, or the signal's long-short decile spread is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent falling unemployment (UNRATE) and rising housing starts (HOUST) signal robust housing market and consumer confidence, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE falls and HOUST rises simultaneously, the 5-day forward return mean is not positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS > 4% and rising), further increases in the fed funds rate compress equity valuations through higher discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS is above 4% and the 12-month change is positive, the 60-day forward return mean is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).
